title: Monday Docs - PDF Export Quality
excerpt:
We are using Monday Docs with dynamic values to generate contracts for our clients. However, when exporting these contracts as PDFs, the formatting is not professional: 

The dynamic values appear inside visible frames, …

posts.author: Ophirp (Ophir Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are using Monday Docs with dynamic values to generate contracts for our clients. However, when exporting these contracts as PDFs, the formatting is not professional:

  • The dynamic values appear inside visible frames, which makes the document look unpolished.
  • The overall PDF formatting is not suitable for sending to external parties.

We would like to understand if there is a way to improve the export quality or an alternative workflow within Monday.com to generate professional-looking contracts.

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is possible with The PDF Maker (app.thepdfmaker.com).

If you’re looking for a more polished and professional way to generate client-facing contracts, The PDF Maker is a great alternative to Monday Docs exports. You can design fully customizable PDF templates with dynamic placeholders (like client name, contract details, values, etc.) and control exactly how the final document appears—no unwanted frames, no layout issues.

You define the formatting, layout, and styling yourself, ensuring the output is consistent with your brand and presentation standards. Whether you’re sending PDFs directly to clients or saving them within your Monday board, The PDF Maker helps you maintain a professional look every time.

Every new response triggers PDF generation automatically.

Please view the support article below:

help.thepdfmaker.com

Automate Documents with the Monday App - Full Guide | The PDF Maker Help Center

Master document automation using the Monday app and PDF Maker. Streamline workflows and enhance productivity by effortlessly generating PDFs directly from Monday.com.

posts.author: Juliia (Juliia)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Ophirp getting professional PDF output from monday Docs is tricky.

What works better for client-facing contracts: start with your actual branded PDF and fill it with monday data, rather than trying to export a Doc into PDF format.

PDF Template Filler lets you upload your existing contract template as a PDF, map your monday fields onto it visually, and generate a clean filled document from any item. No formatting issues, the output looks exactly like your original document.

If you use the same contract structure for different clients, you map the fields once and reuse the template anytime, just generate a new filled PDF per client item.

See how it works (1 min): PDF Template Filler demo

There’s a free plan if you want to test it. Does this approach fit what you have in mind?
